title: Burbank Property Crimes Lawyer - Farris Law Firm
metaDescription: Facing penalties for property crimes in Burbank? Farris Law Firm offers strong legal defense to protect your rights. Call for a free consultation today.
contentHtml:
A property crime is a serious offense with severe legal consequences if the court finds you guilty. These crimes involve unlawfully taking, damaging, or destroying another person’s property without their consent.

From burglary and theft to vandalism and arson, property crimes can range in severity and carry hefty fines, probation, and even jail time.

If you face property crime charges, a lawyer can explain your legal rights and the potential penalties you may face. In fact, hiring an experienced Burbank property crimes lawyer can make all the difference in protecting your freedom and your future—so call Farris Law Firm’s Burbank property crimes lawyers today.

Here are just a few reasons why you should choose Farris Law Firm:

  4. Proven Results: Our track record speaks for itself. We’ve successfully helped countless clients navigate the complex legal system and achieve favorable outcomes, whether that’s a reduced sentence, alternative sentencing, or a complete dismissal of the charges.

Were You Charged with a Property Crime in Burbank?

Are you accused of committing a property crime in the Burbank area? Whether it’s a misdemeanor or a felony, the stakes are high, and you need to take immediate action to safeguard your rights.

Don’t try to navigate the legal system alone – let the skilled attorneys at Farris Law Firm provide the guidance and representation you need.

The Most Common Types of Property Crimes and Penalties

Property crimes come in many forms, each with its own set of penalties. Some of the most common property crimes include:

  1. Burglary: Unlawfully entering a building or structure with the intent to commit a crime. Penalties can range from a misdemeanor with up to one year in county jail to a felony with up to six years in state prison.
  2. Theft: Unlawfully taking or carrying away another’s personal property. Penalties depend on the value of the stolen property, ranging from a misdemeanor with up to one year in county jail to a felony with up to three years in state prison.
  3. Vandalism: Intentionally damaging, defacing, or destroying the property of another. Penalties can range from a misdemeanor with up to one year in county jail to a felony with up to three years in state prison, depending on the amount of damage.
  4. Arson: Intentionally setting fire to or burning any structure, forest land, or property. Arson can be charged as a misdemeanor or a felony, with penalties ranging from 16 months to nine years in state prison.

These are just a few examples of the many property crimes you could be charged with. Regardless of the specific offense, it’s crucial to have a skilled Burbank property crimes lawyer on your side to protect your rights and minimize the potential consequences.

What to Expect From the Prosecution

When you’re facing property crime charges, the prosecution will be working hard to build a case against you. They’ll gather as much evidence as possible, including witness statements, surveillance footage, and any physical evidence found at the scene.

The prosecutor’s goal is to prove beyond a reasonable doubt that you committed the crime. They may try to paint you as a repeat offender or someone who poses a threat to the community. The prosecution has significant resources and legal knowledge at their disposal.

That’s why you need an experienced Burbank property crimes lawyer. We’ll carefully examine the prosecution’s case, identify any weaknesses or inconsistencies, and develop a strong defense strategy to protect your rights and your freedom.

The Standard Courtroom Process for Property Crimes

Here’s a general overview of the stages where our lawyers can help you with property crime charges:

  1. Arraignment: This is your first court appearance, where the state will formally charge you and give you the opportunity to enter a plea of guilty or not guilty.
  2. Preliminary Hearing: If you plead not guilty, the prosecution will present evidence to a judge, who will determine if the evidence merits a trial.
  3. Plea Bargaining: Your Burbank property crimes lawyer may negotiate a plea bargain with the prosecution, resulting in reduced charges or a more lenient sentence.
  4. Trial: If you cannot reach an acceptable plea bargain, your case will proceed to trial, where a judge or jury will hear the evidence and determine your guilt or innocence.
  5. Sentencing: If the court finds you guilty, the judge will determine your sentence, including fines, probation, or incarceration.

Throughout this process, your Farris Law Firm attorney will advocate for you and ensure you receive a fair and just outcome.

How We’ll Build a Strategy to Prove Your Innocence and Protect Your Freedom

At Farris Law Firm, we’re committed to providing you with personalized attention and aggressive representation to protect your rights and future.

Our Burbank property crimes lawyers will work tirelessly to build a strong defense strategy tailored to your case’s unique circumstances. We’ll thoroughly investigate the evidence, challenge the prosecution’s claims, and explore all available legal options to achieve the best possible outcome.

Some of the strategies we may employ include:

  • Challenging the legality of the search and seizure procedures used by law enforcement
  • Questioning the reliability and credibility of witness testimony
  • Presenting evidence that undermines the prosecution’s case
  • Negotiating a plea bargain to reduce the charges or the potential sentence
  • Preparing a compelling case for trial, if necessary, to prove your innocence

Throughout the process, we’ll inform you every step of the way and work closely with you to ensure that your voice is heard and to protect your rights.
